task_completion.description: The Dockerfile is modified to remove PostgreSQL and the legacy startup script, running only the Python app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of the Dockerfile. It should not contain PostgreSQL installation commands or references to a startup script, and should have a CMD or ENTRYPOINT running the Python app directly.

task_completion.description: A docker-compose.yml file is created defining 'app' and 'db' services.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing a docker-compose.yml file with a 'services' block containing both 'app' and 'db'.

task_completion.description: The 'db' service is configured with the postgres:15-alpine image and a named volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the docker-compose.yml file, the 'db' service specifies 'image: postgres:15-alpine' and includes a 'volumes' block referencing a named volume.

task_completion.description: The 'app' service is configured to connect to the 'db' service with the correct credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the docker-compose.yml file (via environment variables) or app.py, the app is configured to use host 'db', user 'notes_user', password 'notes_password', and database 'notes_db'.

task_completion.description: The Docker Compose stack is successfully started.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'docker compose up' (or similar) with both 'app' and 'db' containers running, or the Docker Desktop GUI showing the active containers.

task_completion.description: The refactored application is accessible on port 5000.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the application loaded at http://localhost:5000, or a successful 'curl http://localhost:5000' command in the terminal returning the app's HTML.

integrity.description: Files were genuinely edited in the project directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths in the text editor or terminal commands match /home/ga/legacy_project/ and are not just scratchpads or echoed text.

integrity.description: Docker Compose was actually executed to start the services.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard Docker Compose output is visible in the terminal (e.g., pulling images, creating networks, starting containers) or the containers are visible in Docker Desktop.

integrity.description: The application response was not mocked or hardcoded.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser or curl output shows a genuine connection to localhost:5000, not a printed string or a local file opened in the browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is /home/ga/legacy_project. The Docker Compose stack must define services named 'app' and 'db'. The 'db' service must use the 'postgres:15-alpine' image. The application must be accessible on port 5000.
